metric_value: Majority still client-server but rapid shift to Web underway
notes: Aberdeen: change in opinion toward Web 'occurred in just the last six months and is rapidly gathering momentum'

metric_value: Web/Internet-native will replace client-server
notes: Aberdeen: 'the future of CIS will be closely intertwined with the Web'; suppliers ignoring Web 'do so at their peril'

metric_name: Web front-end vs. native Web CIS differentiation
metric_value: Web front-end ≠ Web-native application; requires two separate systems
notes: Aberdeen: hybrid Web/client-server offerings require separate systems; 'over the long term applications built entirely for the Web will take over'

metric_value: User organizations increasingly demand CIS-to-ERP integration
notes: EBA universe: CIS + ERP + e-commerce + decision support; Aberdeen: integration capabilities increasingly demanded during vendor evaluation

metric_value: 2-5 years for complete CIS deployment
notes: Typically deploy single module first; integration of multiple CIS applications takes 2-5 years; Aberdeen field research finding

metric_value: CIS suppliers will announce decision support alliances or capabilities within 18 months
notes: Aberdeen predicts CIS+decision support alliances within 18 months of May 1998; data mining of CIS-generated customer data

metric_value: 10% sales increase = 3x entire IS budget impact for most corporations

metric_value: Entered CIS market with Front Office suites (OSM

metric_value: First enterprise-scale Web-native CIS solution (eService)

metric_value: Smaller suppliers will exit or be acquired; users will buy only from market leaders

metric_value: Support group is usually first to deploy CIS (before SFA or quality control)

metric_value: Top supplier of internal help desk applications

metric_value: CTI critical success factor for telemarketing/telesales applications

metric_value: CIS generates revenues; ERP/financials reduce costs — CIS impact is order-of-magnitude greater
